doctor_description: Solid subpleural nodule 5mm image 191, not previously visualized. Impression: CT in 12 months per Fleischner.
management_impact: Validator does not confirm the new RLL nodule; validator recommends LDCT annually per Lung-RADS Cat 1 for the stable RUL nodule. No separate CT triggered for RLL.
notes: Doctor calls new RLL 5mm nodule and recommends 12m CT. Validator omits this nodule and manages with LDCT. No net management change — LDCT covers surveillance.

doctor_description: Confluent centrilobular emphysema documented in findings and impression; no LDCT lung cancer screening language.
validator_description: Severe centrilobular emphysema — independent lung cancer risk factor. Consider LDCT enrollment if eligible; today's CT may serve as baseline. Annual LDCT per Lung-RADS Cat 1.
management_impact: Doctor omits QMM23-required LDCT screening recommendation for eligible patients with emphysema. Validator explicitly recommends LDCT enrollment.
notes: Classic QMM23 miss. Doctor documents emphysema but omits independent lung cancer risk statement and LDCT recommendation.

doctor_description: Left renal collecting system dilatation (left hydronephrosis only).
validator_description: Bilateral hydronephrosis.
management_impact: Doctor misses right-sided hydronephrosis. Bilateral vs unilateral has different clinical implications but no additional imaging specifically triggered from CT report alone.
notes: Doctor reports only left hydronephrosis; validator identifies bilateral. Underreported laterality.

validator_description: Mild enlargement of the left ventricle.
management_impact: No immediate management change — incidental finding not independently actionable on chest CT alone.
notes: Doctor omits mild LV enlargement noted by validator. Incidental but reportable finding.

validator_description: Multiple low-density rib lesions (images 117, 119, 124), probably fat, may correspond to extramedullary hematopoiesis, unchanged. Context: polycythemia referral.
management_impact: No immediate management change — findings are unchanged and clinically contextualized by polycythemia diagnosis. Documentation gap.
notes: Clinically relevant in polycythemia context. Doctor omits entirely. No additional imaging triggered.

doctor_description: Severe CAC in LM, LAD, LCx, RCA. PCP consultation recommended.
validator_description: Severe CAC in LM, LAD, LCx, RCA. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: None — both concordant including ACRad44-compliant PCP recommendation.
notes: ACRad44 satisfied by both reports.

doctor_description: LUL solid mass 2.6×1.5×2.6cm stable vs Oct 10 2025. No follow-up interval recommended in impression.
validator_description: LUL nodule 2.6×1.5×2.6cm stable. Per Fleischner: follow-up CT at 18–24 months after initial discovery.
management_impact: Doctor completely omits Fleischner follow-up recommendation for a 2.6cm LUL nodule. Validator correctly states CT 18-24 months. Known/stable nodule of this size requires ongoing surveillance — omission fails to direct referring physician.
notes: Management-changing omission: no follow-up recommendation for a 2.6cm pulmonary nodule. Measure 364 violation.

doctor_description: Moderate CAC documented in findings. Impression mentions calcification of coronary arteries, mitral valve, and thoracic aorta — no explicit PCP consultation recommendation.
validator_description: Moderate CAC. Explicit PCP consultation recommendation for preventive risk modification or quantitative assessment.
management_impact: ACRad44 requires explicit PCP referral language. Doctor omits the required recommendation. May not prompt PCP referral.
notes: ACRad44 MIPS violation — PCP recommendation absent from impression.

doctor_description: Mitral valve calcification only.
validator_description: Mitral and aortic valve calcification.
management_impact: No management change — incidental valve calcification; no actionable consequence from chest CT alone.
notes: Doctor documents mitral valve calcification but misses aortic valve calcification.

validator_description: Fatty inclusions of the myocardium of the ventricles consistent with coronary artery disease.
management_impact: No management change — incidental finding consistent with known CAD context.
notes: Fatty myocardial inclusions omitted by doctor. Clinically contextual finding with no immediate management impact.

doctor_description: Moderate degenerative changes. No vertebral height loss mentioned.
validator_description: Decrease in T6 vertebral body height by 26.0% (Genant 2) — sign of osteoporosis consistent with prior bone density testing.
management_impact: A Genant 2 (moderate) vertebral compression fracture is a clinically significant finding that directly impacts osteoporosis management — confirms severity requiring treatment. Doctor completely misses this fracture.
notes: New or previously uncharacterized Genant 2 compression fracture at T6. Management-changing: osteoporosis grading and treatment escalation indicated.

doctor_description: LUL solid nodule 4mm partially calcified (image 162).
validator_description: LUL ground glass nodule 7mm (image 73) — additional lesion not described by doctor.
management_impact: In a patient with active prostate cancer (C61), a 7mm GGN in LUL is a clinically significant finding requiring oncology follow-up protocol. Doctor completely misses this nodule. Management change: oncology surveillance required.
notes: Doctor documents LUL solid 4mm (image 162) but misses separate GGN 7mm (image 73). In oncology context, all nodules require specific characterization and protocol-based follow-up.

finding_category: Nodule follow-up protocol — Fleischner vs oncology
doctor_description: If risk factors present, CT 12 months. If no risk factors, per Fleischner no follow-up.
validator_description: Fleischner Society recommendations may not be applicable considering the clinical history (prostate cancer C61). Follow-up per oncology follow-up protocol.
management_impact: Doctor applies standard Fleischner protocol to a patient with active prostate cancer. Validator correctly defers to oncology follow-up protocol. In cancer patients, standard Fleischner is superseded — management change: oncology protocol required.
notes: Wrong follow-up framework for oncology patient. This is a systematic error that affects all nodule management in this study.

doctor_description: Aortic valve calcification noted.
validator_description: Mild calcifications of the mitral and aortic valves.
management_impact: No management change — incidental valve calcification.
notes: Doctor documents aortic valve calcification but misses mitral valve calcification.

doctor_description: Parenchymal calcification upper third left kidney (partially included): neoplastic changes unlikely but cannot be excluded.
validator_description: Cyst of the left kidney with calcification nearby. ACR recommendation may not be applicable considering clinical history (prostate cancer).
management_impact: Different characterization (calcification vs cyst). Validator appropriately notes ACR guidelines may not apply in oncology context. No immediate management change but framing differs.
notes: Both identify left kidney lesion. Doctor uncertain about calcification vs neoplasm; validator identifies cyst with nearby calcification and flags oncology caveat.

doctor_description: Severe CAC. PCP consultation recommended.
validator_description: Severe CAC. PCP consultation recommended.
management_impact: None — ACRad44 satisfied by both.
notes: Both concordant on severe CAC with PCP recommendation.

doctor_description: Moderate coronary calcification. PCP recommendation stated.
validator_description: Severe CAC in LM, LAD, LCx, and RCA (all 4 vessels). PCP recommendation stated.
management_impact: PCP recommendation present in both — ACRad44 technically met. However, severity misgrading (moderate vs severe) may affect PCP's risk stratification decision.
notes: ACRad44 PCP referral present in both; severity discrepancy (moderate vs severe — all 4 vessels) is clinically meaningful but does not block PCP referral.

doctor_description: LUL subsolid + LLL GGO unchanged. Per Fleischner: CT 3–6 months confirming stability, then annual CT × 5 years.
validator_description: Stable GGN up to 6mm. Per Fleischner: CT every 2 years until 5 years after initial discovery.
management_impact: Doctor recommends 3-6m CT then annual — more aggressive than Fleischner 2017 for GGN ≤6mm (biennial). Validator correctly applies biennial protocol. Doctor's recommendation triggers more frequent imaging than indicated.
notes: Fleischner 2017 for pure GGN ≤6mm: no routine follow-up or biennial. Doctor's annual surveillance is incorrect. Measure 364 violation — wrong interval.

doctor_description: Aortic valve calcification noted.
management_impact: No management change — incidental valve finding not confirmed by validator.
notes: Doctor documents aortic valve calcification not mentioned by validator. Minor overcall with no management consequence.

doctor_description: Diffuse adrenal gland thickening up to 1.1cm, no change.
management_impact: No management change — doctor notes it as unchanged; validator omits. Stable finding with no actionable consequence.
notes: Doctor documents adrenal thickening not confirmed by validator. Stable and unchanged — no management impact.

doctor_description: RUL mixed GGN 1.6cm — no change. Follow-up CT in 6 months.
validator_description: RUL GGN with spicules, cords to pleura, bronchiectasis: 1.9×0.6cm (prev 1.8×0.6) and 1.7×1.7cm (prev 1.6×1.4). Growth more pronounced vs Apr 2024 LDCT. May correspond to slowly growing adenocarcinoma.
management_impact: Doctor misses documented interval growth and suspicious morphologic features (spiculation, pleural tethering, internal bronchiectasis). Validator raises adenocarcinoma concern. Management change: PET/CT or biopsy warranted vs simple 6-month CT follow-up.
notes: Critical miss: doctor states 'no change' for a nodule that has grown in both dimensions with new spiculation and pleural tethering. Measure 364 — wrong follow-up recommendation.

doctor_description: Moderate CAC documented. No PCP recommendation in impression.
validator_description: Moderate CAC in LAD and RCA. PCP recommendation explicitly stated.
management_impact: ACRad44 requires explicit PCP referral language. Doctor omits it from impression.
notes: ACRad44 MIPS violation — PCP recommendation absent.

validator_description: Solid nodule 4mm image 83, unchanged.
management_impact: Stable 4mm solid nodule — per Fleischner no follow-up required. Documentation gap with no management impact.
notes: Doctor misses stable LUL 4mm solid nodule. No follow-up change.

validator_description: Poorly defined centrilobular GGOs and mucous contents of small bronchi — smoking-related respiratory bronchiolitis.
management_impact: No immediate management change — incidental smoking-related finding. Documentation gap.
notes: Clinically relevant in smoking history context. No additional imaging triggered.

validator_description: Haller index 3.5 — pectus excavatum.
management_impact: No immediate management change — structural finding. Haller index 3.5 is borderline for surgical consideration but requires clinical correlation.
notes: Reportable structural finding omitted by doctor.

doctor_description: Single liver lesion 6mm <20 HU — no follow-up per ACR White Paper 2017 (benign, likely cyst).
validator_description: Single liver lesion 6mm. For low-risk patients: no follow-up. For high-risk patients: follow-up MRI 3–6 months.
management_impact: Doctor blanket-applies no follow-up; validator correctly risk-stratifies. In a smoker being evaluated for a potentially malignant nodule, the high-risk liver MRI pathway may be relevant.
notes: Risk stratification omitted by doctor. Validator correctly provides conditional recommendation. No definitive management change but framing differs.
